title: OCD-Fighting Lifestyle Checklist
description: Track the lifestyle practices that support OCD recovery and complement professional treatment. These don't replace ERP, but they create conditions where recovery is easier and relapse is less likely.
instructions: Review each item and check off the practices you've done this week. Focus on what you can realistically maintain — even a few checked items is meaningful. Revisit weekly to track patterns and progress.

items.label: I maintained consistent sleep and wake times most days this week
I followed a calming wind-down routine before bed (not rituals — genuine relaxation)
I maintained a basic daily routine (meals, activities, rest)
I did some form of physical activity or movement this week
I ate regular meals and stayed hydrated
I kept caffeine at a moderate level (not using it to push through exhaustion)
I did not use alcohol or substances to cope with OCD distress
I resisted at least one compulsion this week (even a small one)
I labeled an intrusive thought as 'just OCD' instead of engaging with it
I reduced reassurance-seeking (asked fewer times, or caught myself before asking)
I faced something I usually avoid because of OCD (even something small)
I completed my ERP homework from therapy (if currently in treatment)
I connected with a supportive person (friend, family, support group)
I spent time on something meaningful or values-aligned (not OCD-driven)
I used a stress management technique (breathing, nature, mindfulness, creative activity)
I practiced self-compassion — acknowledged that OCD is hard and I'm doing my best

disclaimer: This checklist supports recovery alongside professional OCD treatment (ERP). It does not replace therapy. If your OCD symptoms are severe or worsening, please seek help from an OCD-trained therapist. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
